input_papers: /Users/saichetannandikanti/Desktop/CAESAR/CultivarDB-RAG/input_papers/Agrosystems Geosci   Env - 2024 - Kipkulei - Agronomic management response in maize  Zea mays L   production across three (1).pdf

records.location: Trans Nzoia County, Uasin Gishu County, Katuke, Sabwani, Olngatongo

data.average_yield_kg_ha: AEZ II: 5312 kg/ha; least feasible yield in study area: 4400 kg/ha; yield in Uasin Gishu County (government statistics): 3700 kg/ha (for comparison, not H614-specific)
data.growing_degree_days: requires more heat units to attain anthesis and reach physiological maturity than H612
data.agro_ecological_zone: AEZ I (humid), AEZ II (subhumid), AEZ III (semihumid), AEZ IV (transitional zone, not main focus)
data.adaptation_notes: H614 performed better than H612 across all AEZs, with reduced likelihood of yield decline. Early and late sowing (especially early March and early May) are optimal, with AEZ II showing highest yields. Early sowing benefits eastern and northwestern AEZ II; late sowing benefits southern Uasin Gishu and Trans Nzoia. AEZ I has poorly drained soils and low temperatures, limiting yield; AEZ III has low rainfall and poor soils. H614 is suitable for Kenyan highlands and is popular among farmers for high production potential.
data.normal_planting_window: Early March and early May preferable in AEZ II and III; AEZ I: sowing window is large, between early March and mid-March and early May.
data.major_crop_areas: Trans Nzoia County, Uasin Gishu County, eastern and northwestern AEZ II, southern Uasin Gishu, western Trans Nzoia, northern Uasin Gishu
